generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor that converts electrical current (I_M) into torque (τ_M) for the MABI MAX 100 first axis. Operates within a restricted range of [-90°, 90°] and max angular velocity of |q̇_max| = 75°/s. No direct digital twin control interaction."
]

instance GearboxTransmission : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical transmission system comprising gearwheel and cycloidal gearbox that transmits torque from the motor to the output shaft while introducing backlash and elasticity effects."
]

instance Sinumerik840DslController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller that processes position, speed, and acceleration inputs/outputs for the MABI MAX 100 robot. Acts as intermediary system for derived data computation using manufacturer-specific parameters."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor providing real-time angular position data (r_M) for friction and elasticity analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance SecondaryPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted behind the gearbox providing real-time output angle data (r_out) for backlash and elasticity analysis by comparing with motor encoder readings."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring motor input current (I_M) for torque estimation (τ_M) using manufacturer-provided parameters."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
]

instance Sinumerik840DslTimeSeries : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller providing time-series data (position, velocity, acceleration) via MQTT streaming for derived data computation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SinumerikTimeSeriesDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]

instance SecondaryPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryPositionEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance SinumerikTimeSeriesDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom Sinumerik840DslTimeSeries
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLBlockDefinitionDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) describing the structural composition of the MABI MAX 100 first axis including motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram (IBD) defining flow definitions for rotational energy (torque/angular velocity) and electrical energy (current) within the axis system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box analytical friction model implementing Coulomb-Viscous friction characteristics for real-time friction identification."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box analytical friction model implementing Stribeck effect characteristics for real-time friction identification."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance DahlFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box analytical friction model implementing Dahl friction characteristics for real-time friction identification."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White-box analytical friction model implementing LuGre model characteristics for real-time friction identification."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance BaseSystemSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink model representing base system behavior without friction effects for comparison with friction models."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance MotorOutputSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component-specific Simulink simulation modeling motor output characteristics including torque conversion."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance GearwheelOutputSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component-specific Simulink simulation modeling gearwheel output characteristics including transmission ratios."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance GearboxOutputSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component-specific Simulink simulation modeling cycloidal gearbox output characteristics including backlash effects."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance MeasuredMotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series array of floating-point values representing measured motor input current (I_M) for torque estimation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance MeasuredMotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series array of floating-point values representing measured motor output angle (r_M) from primary encoder."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance MeasuredOutputPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series array of floating-point values representing measured output angle (r_out) from secondary encoder."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance MeasuredTimeSeriesData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series array of floating-point values representing position, velocity, and acceleration data from SINUMERIK controller via MQTT."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SinumerikTimeSeriesDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance SimulatedMotorTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series array of floating-point values representing simulated motor torque (τ_M) derived from friction models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorOutputSimulation
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

instance SimulatedMotorVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series array of floating-point values representing simulated motor angular velocity (ω_M) with friction compensation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorOutputSimulation
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

instance SimulatedGearwheelVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series array of floating-point values representing simulated gearwheel angular velocity (ω_G) with friction compensation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearwheelOutputSimulation
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

instance SimulatedOutputVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series array of floating-point values representing simulated output angular velocity (ω_out) with friction compensation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxOutputSimulation
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

instance TorqueVelocityDeviationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series array of floating-point values representing deviations between simulated and measured torque/velocity data for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DataComparisonEnabler
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML modeling environment for creating Block Definition Diagrams (BDDs) and Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs) describing structural and behavioral models of the physical system."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService, DeviationAnalysisService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks MATLAB Simulink environment for executing friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) and system simulations."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService, ModelRefinementService, VisualizationService
]

instance DataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom processing components consuming input data traces (I_M, r_M, r_G) and producing refined outputs including torque/velocity calculations."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviationAnalysisService, ModelRefinementService
]

instance MQTTDataStreamingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation for real-time transmission of sensor data (current, angle) from SINUMERIK controller to digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService, DeviationAnalysisService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Integrated dashboard displaying data traces (I_M, r_M, r_G, r_out) and simulated outputs (δr_M, δr_G, δr_out) for multi-physics-aware analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance DataComparisonEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing component comparing simulated vs. measured data (torque, angular velocity) to detect friction losses and backlash effects."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviationAnalysisService
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionIdentificationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous, real-time identification of joint friction models using live data and simulation models (white-box: analytical models like Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck; black-box: data-driven models)."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionModelInsight, AdaptiveControlAction
]

instance DeviationAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Comparison of simulated vs. measured data (torque, angular velocity) to detect friction losses and backlash effects in real-time."
    DTDFVocab:provides TorqueDeviationInsight, OperationalMonitoringAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integrated dashboard displaying data traces (I_M, r_M, r_G, r_out) and simulated outputs (δr_M, δr_G, δr_out) for multi-physics-aware analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance ModelRefinementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic exchange of friction models to adapt to operational changes (e.g., wear, tool variations) via Simulink simulations consuming input data traces."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelRefinementInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionModelInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified joint friction models (white-box: analytical; black-box: data-driven) for real-time friction compensation."
]

instance TorqueDeviationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detected deviations between simulated and measured torque/velocity data indicating friction losses and backlash effects."
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Multi-physics-aware visualization of data traces and simulated outputs for operational analysis."
]

instance ModelRefinementInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Refined friction models dynamically exchanged to adapt to operational changes (wear, tool variations)."
]

instance AdaptiveControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adaptive control strategies implemented to mitigate motion inaccuracies (up to 30%) caused by joint friction."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance OperationalMonitoringAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of torque-velocity deviations enabling operators to detect anomalies (e.g., sudden friction spikes) and adjust operational parameters."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C18: Environment/Component
instance MabiMax100FirstAxis : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "First vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot comprising permanent magnet synchronous motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox."
    base:contains MotorActuator, GearboxTransmission, Sinumerik840DslController, MotorPositionEncoder, SecondaryPositionEncoder, MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance DigitalShadowFriction : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Centralized digital shadow (FrictionShadow) serving as primary twin for the MABI MAX 100 first axis friction analysis."
    base:contains SysMLBlockDefinitionDiagram, SysMLInternalBlockDiagram, CoulombViscousFrictionModel, StribeckFrictionModel, DahlFrictionModel, LuGreFrictionModel, BaseSystemSimulation, MotorOutputSimulation, GearwheelOutputSimulation, GearboxOutputSimulation
]

instance ProcessingLayer : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Decentralized processing components orchestrated under unified digital shadow framework including simulation models for motor, gearwheel, and gearbox."
    base:contains SimulinkSimulationEnabler, DataProcessingEnabler, DataComparisonEnabler
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates in **real-time** with **millisecond-level synchronization** for live data processing. The friction models are continuously updated during operation, requiring **low-latency data ingestion** (MQTT streaming) and **high-frequency computation** (e.g., torque/velocity calculations from encoder data). The twinning process aligns with the **operational time-scale** of the robot (e.g., machining tasks) to ensure accuracy in motion control adjustments."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation is structured as a **purpose-driven digital shadow** following the **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)**. Key components include:
  - **Purpose**: Friction derivation and motion accuracy improvement (defined by the singleton class *FrictionDerivation*).
  - **Asset**: The MABI robot’s first axis (*Mabi*), described via SysML *SystemModel* and simulation models (*SimModel_M*, *SimModel_GW*, *SimModel_GB*).
  - **Data Sources**: Physical sensors (encoders, current sensors) and virtual sources (simulation outputs).
  - **Processing Layer**: Decoupled **processing components** (Simulation M, Simulation GW, Simulation GB) that execute friction models and derive simulated outputs.
  - **Visualization**: Unified dashboard integrating **data traces** (measured/simulated) for multi-physics analysis.
  The constellation emphasizes **traceability** (data origins, model dependencies) and **purpose-specific aggregation** of data."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin covers the **operational phase** of the physical twin's life cycle, focusing on **as-operated** behavior (real-time monitoring and analysis). It does not model the **as-designed** (ideal) or **as-manufactured** phases explicitly, though **engineering models** (SysML) and **simulation models** (Simulink) provide structural and behavioral representations aligned with the operational scope. The DS is designed to **continuously improve** during operation via live data integration."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Model Validation**: Comparison of **simulated vs. measured data** (e.g., torque, angular velocity) to quantify friction-induced deviations.
  - **Uncertainty Quantification**: Analysis of encoder discrepancies (motor vs. secondary encoder) to assess backlash and elasticity effects.
  - **Parameter Sensitivity**: Friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck) are validated against empirical data to refine parameters dynamically.
  - **Verification Mechanisms**: Traceability of data traces to their **sources** (MQTT channels, sensors) and **processing components** (Simulink simulations) ensures reproducibility. The DS does not explicitly address **uncertainty propagation** or **error bounds** in the documents."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical twin (PT) and digital twin (DT) involves:
  - **Data Protocols**: **MQTT** for real-time streaming of sensor data (current, angle) from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller to the digital shadow.
  - **Network Architecture**: Implicit **industrial Ethernet** or similar infrastructure for low-latency communication, though specifics are not detailed.
  - **Data Formats**: Time-series arrays of **floating-point values** (torque τ, angular velocity ω) transmitted as structured data traces.
  - **Synchronization**: Aligned with the **operational clock** of the robot (e.g., machining cycles) to ensure temporal coherence."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin adheres to the following standards and frameworks:
  - **Modeling Standards**: **SysML** (OMG standard) for structural and behavioral modeling of the physical system.
  - **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)**: Customized reference model (UML class diagram) for digital shadow design, based on prior research (e.g., Becker et al., Michael et al.).
  - **Simulation Standards**: **MathWorks Simulink** (industry-standard for dynamic system simulation) for friction model implementation.
  - **Data Exchange**: **MQTT** (OASIS standard) for real-time data streaming from the CNC controller.
  - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: Implied for **contextual metadata management** (e.g., system properties) though not explicitly standardized (e.g., Industry 4.0 AAS compliance is not confirmed)."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The documents do not explicitly address **cybersecurity** or **safety protocols**, but the following can be inferred:
  - **Safety Constraints**: The robot’s **safety cell** restricts the working range to [-90°, 90°] and maximum speed (75°/s), implying **hardware-level safety mechanisms**.
  - **Data Integrity**: MQTT streaming and sensor data are used for **diagnostic purposes only**; no remote actuation of the PT is described, reducing safety risks.
  - **Potential Risks**: Lack of explicit discussion on **data encryption**, **authentication**, or **access control** for the digital shadow’s dashboard or simulation models. The DS is designed for **monitoring and analysis**, not for **critical control operations**, which mitigates some safety concerns but does not address **remote emergency interventions** or **human-machine interaction safety**."
]
